registry_metadata.name: Trusted Data Source Registry
registry_metadata.purpose: Whitelist of pre-vetted data sources the dashboard may pull from directly (ingestion method 'trusted_source'). Each source is authoritative ONLY for the fields listed against it. The ingestion agent must not use a source for a field it is not registered for. Method-3 web-gap candidates that originate from a domain in this registry may be ranked as higher-confidence, but still require PR approval.
registry_metadata.owner: TWG / project lead — add or remove sources here; changes take effect without touching the field schema.
registry_metadata.rule: For ingestion_method='trusted_source', trusted_source_id MUST be one of the ids below AND the field_id being populated MUST appear in that source's authoritative_for list.

sources.name: ITC Trade Map
sources.notes: Import value, export value, and derived trade balance / import-dependency. Bilateral and product-level trade statistics.

sources.name: Uganda Bureau of Statistics
sources.access: Published statistical abstracts / API where available
sources.notes: Domestic production volume/value, firm counts, employment, productivity. National authority for Uganda industrial statistics.

sources.name: UN Comtrade
sources.notes: Cross-check / fallback for trade data where Trade Map coverage is thin.

sources.name: World Integrated Trade Solution (World Bank)
sources.notes: Trade flows plus tariff/competitiveness context for regional benchmarking.

sources.name: Bank of Uganda
sources.notes: Lending rates / cost of credit context for the finance domain.

sources.name: UNIDO Statistics (INDSTAT / MVA)
sources.notes: Manufacturing value added, productivity, value-addition share, international comparators.

extension_guidance: To add a source: give it a stable id, list only the field_ids it is genuinely authoritative for, set a default_confidence, and record access method. Do not list a source for a field outside its competence (e.g. a trade database is not authoritative for capacity utilization).
